titleStmt.title: Letter from George Gavan Duffy to Ernest Blythe, 21 April 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: George Gavan Duffy

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from George Gavan Duffy (1882-1951) to Ernest Blythe (1889-1975). Gavan Duffy writes after Blythe had been released from prison and asks Blythe to send him a copy of the local paper if it includes a report of his court case. George Gavan Duffy was an Irish politician, barrister and judge. He unsuccessfully defended Roger Casement at his trial for high treason after the Easter Rising. Gavan Duffy had been acting for Ernest Blythe, organiser for the Irish Volunteers. Blythe had been arrested under the Defence of the Realm Act. He was released from prison in England before the Easter Rising but was rearrested there during Easter week 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Bureau of Military History Contemporary Documents, George Gavan Duffy Collection, BMH CD/45/3/11/6

correspAction.placeName: 18 St. Helen's Street, Abingdon, Berkshire, England

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
21st April 1916. Mr Ernest Blythe 18 St Helen's Street Abingdon. Berks. A chara:â I shall be very glad if you send me a copy of
the legal paper for tomorrow's date if it contains an
account of your case. My address is Glenvar, Near Letterkenny. Mise do chara, G. Gavan Duffy .
